Our practice

A space where you don't have to explain yourself.

At Houston Heights Therapy, we work with people who have often felt like they had to shrink themselves to fit — in other spaces, other relationships, other therapists' offices. You don't have to do that here.

We specialize in trauma, LGBTQ+ affirming care, and neurodivergent-welcoming therapy. Our clinicians bring warmth, clinical depth, and a genuine commitment to meeting you where you are.

Meet our therapists →

What we offer

Services tailored to you.

🧠

Individual Therapy

Practical, affirming support for anxiety, ADHD, trauma, identity, and life transitions. In-person and telehealth.

Learn more → 💙

EMDR & Trauma Therapy

Evidence-based trauma processing that helps integrate difficult experiences and reduce trauma responses — grounded in neuroscience.

Learn more → 🏳️‍🌈

LGBTQ+ & Gender-Affirming

Care that centers your identity without requiring you to educate your therapist. Affirming across the full spectrum of gender and sexuality.

Learn more → 🤝

Group Therapy

Structured groups for connection, recovery, and shared growth. A more approachable entry point into therapeutic space.

See current groups → ⚡

Therapy Intensives

Extended sessions for deeper, faster progress — ideal for those who want to do focused work in a concentrated format.

Learn more → 📋

Rates & Insurance

We accept Aetna, BCBS, and Cigna. Self-pay $80/session. Sliding scale available. HSA/FSA accepted.

See full details →

Getting started

Three steps. No guesswork.

01

Tell us about yourself

Fill out our short consultation request form. It takes about 3 minutes and helps us make sure we're the right fit before you ever book a time.

02

Book your free consultation

After submitting, you'll go straight to our scheduling page to pick a time for your free 20-minute consultation — no waiting for a callback.

03

Start therapy

Meet your therapist, complete a short intake, and begin. Sessions are available in-person at our Houston Heights office or via telehealth.

Get started — free 20-min consult

Your therapists

People you'll actually want to talk to.

Hannah Drury

LMSW · Supervised by Joe Lenhoff, LCSW-S

Hannah works with adults and adolescents (13+) navigating trauma, LGBTQ+ identity, addiction, and anxiety. Affirming, trauma-informed, and skilled in CBT, DBT, and EMDR.

Trauma & PTSD LGBTQ+ Affirming EMDR Adolescents (13+)

Ron Youngblut

LMSW · Supervised by Joe Lenhoff, LCSW-S

Ron specializes in trauma, addiction recovery, veterans' services, and major life transitions. Grounded and nonjudgmental, integrating ACT, CBT, and The Daring Way.

Trauma & PTSD Veterans Addiction Recovery The Daring Way

Joe Lenhoff

LCSW-S · Founder & Clinical Supervisor (TX #68290)

Joe is the founder and clinical supervisor of Houston Heights Therapy. He provides supervision to associate clinicians and is not currently accepting new clients through the website.

IFS EMDR Clinical Supervisor

Learn about our groups

Our mission

Affordable, affirming, trauma-engaged care.

We provide care for individuals, couples, and groups — with a particular focus on LGBTQIA+ clients, neurodivergent individuals, people of color, and those impacted by trauma.

Every person who comes to us deserves to feel seen, valued, and supported — not managed, not pathologized, not asked to fit a mold. That's the practice we've built.

Our office is in Houston Heights, and we offer telehealth across Texas for those who prefer to meet online or can't make it in person.

About our practice Rates & insurance

Ready when you are.

There's no perfect moment to start therapy. If you're here, that's enough. The consultation is free, it's 20 minutes, and there's no obligation.

If you are in crisis: Houston Heights Therapy is not a crisis service and our website is not monitored 24/7. If you are in emotional distress or thinking about suicide, please call or text 988 to connect with the Suicide & Crisis Lifeline, or go to your nearest emergency department.
© 2026 Houston Heights Therapy · Client Rights & Policies Hannah Drury and Ron Youngblut are Licensed Master Social Workers practicing under the supervision of Joe Lenhoff, LCSW-S (TX #68290).
